top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Istria Granfondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
5,725ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
69 miles on coastal flats exploding into brutal Istrian hills burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Istria Granfondo's coastal flats exploding into brutal Istrian hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 69 miles.
82ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Istria Granfondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for Istria Granfondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,725ft of climbing on coastal flats exploding into brutal Istrian hills does to your power file on race day.
At Istria Granfondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on coastal flats exploding into brutal Istrian hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Istria Granfondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Istria Granfondo’s 69 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Istria Granfondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Durability to prepare you for 69 miles of coastal flats exploding into brutal istrian hills in Umag, Istria County, Croatia.
